## [Ralph] [PLAN] Round 1 | Step: planning
**Time**: 2026-03-25 03:14:37
**Summary**: 

[PLAN]

## Task Understanding

Implement changes to 4 Apprise notification plugins to pass 11 failing tests across 4 files:

1. **Nextcloud** (7 tests): Add group targets (`#devs`), all-users target (`all`), direct-user `@` prefix, caching, deduplication, and OCS API resolution.
2. **Resend** (2 tests): Rename `from_email` param to `from_addr`, extend `parse_url` for `apikey`/`from`/`name`/`reply` query params.
3. **SendGrid** (1 test case): Don't fallback to from_email when explicit targets were all invalid.
4. **Slack** (1 test, multiple cases): Add `mode` query param support, `SlackMode.HOOK`/`GOV_HOOK`, gov-cloud webhook URL, mode enforcement.

## Proposed Approach

### Requirement 1: Nextcloud (`nextcloud.py`)
- Add `_resolved_cache` dict in `__init__` for caching group/all lookups
- In `send()`, expand targets: `#group` → OCS group API, `all` → OCS users API, `@user`/plain → direct user
- Deduplicate resolved usernames before POSTing
- If any group/all resolution fails (non-200 or exception), return False immediately (no POSTs)
- In `url()`, serialize with `@` prefix for direct users, `#` for groups, `all` as-is
- In `parse_url()`, preserve `@`/`#`/`all` prefixes from URL path

### Requirement 2: Resend (`resend.py`)
- Rename `from_email` parameter to `from_addr` in `__init__`
- In `parse_url()`:
  - Fall back to `qsd["apikey"]` when user/password don't yield an API key
  - Fall back to `qsd["from"]` for from address (parse RFC 5322 display name)
  - Support `name` and `reply` query params, store on instance
- In `url()`: include `name` and `reply` in query string when set

### Requirement 3: SendGrid (`sendgrid.py`)
- Track whether caller provided explicit targets (`_has_explicit_targets`)
- Only fallback to `from_email` when no targets were specified at all
- In `send()`, return False if `self.targets` is empty

### Requirement 4: Slack (`slack.py`)
- Add `SlackMode.HOOK = "hook"` and `SlackMode.GOV_HOOK = "gov-hook"` to SlackMode
- Add both to `SLACK_MODES`
- Accept `mode` kwarg in `__init__`; enforce: if mode=bot but only webhook tokens → raise TypeError
- Map HOOK to existing webhook behavior, GOV_HOOK to `hooks.slack-gov.com`
- In `parse_url()`: read `mode` from qsd and pass through
- In `url()`: include `mode=hook` or `mode=gov-hook` for webhook modes
- In `parse_native_url()`: handle `hooks.slack-gov.com` → set mode=gov-hook; existing `hooks.slack.com` → set mode=hook
- In `send()`: use gov-cloud URL when mode is GOV_HOOK

## Expected Deliverables
- Modified: `nextcloud.py`, `resend.py`, `sendgrid.py`, `slack.py`
- No test file modifications

## Test Plan
- **Test command**: `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q`
- **Expected coverage**: All 11 acceptance tests pass, plus no regressions in existing tests
- Specific test files: `test_plugin_nextcloud.py`, `test_plugin_resend.py`, `test_plugin_sendgrid.py`, `test_plugin_slack.py`

## Quality Gate Commands
- `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q`

## [Lisa] [NEEDS_WORK] Round 1 | Step: planning
**Time**: 2026-03-25 03:16:31

Direction is aligned with the task, but this round is not reviewable as an implementation submission. `work.md:3`-`work.md:62` contains only a `[PLAN]`, with no `[CODE]`/`[FIX]` section, no Files Changed list, and no Test Results block to verify against the required Docker command.

The targeted code paths are also still in their pre-requirement state:
- `code/apprise/plugins/nextcloud.py:197` iterates over stored targets and POSTs them directly as `{target}` at `code/apprise/plugins/nextcloud.py:235`, so the required `#group` / `all` OCS resolution and `@user` normalization are not implemented.
- `code/apprise/plugins/resend.py:145` still takes `from_email`, and `code/apprise/plugins/resend.py:250` still serializes `{from_email}` without the required `from_addr` / query-param handling described in the task.
- `code/apprise/plugins/sendgrid.py:255` still auto-falls back to `self.from_email` when no valid targets survive validation, which is exactly the behavior the requirement says to stop for explicit-but-invalid targets.
- `code/apprise/plugins/slack.py:105`-`code/apprise/plugins/slack.py:117` only define `webhook` and `bot` modes, and `code/apprise/plugins/slack.py:343` hard-selects mode from token presence, so the required `mode=hook` / `mode=gov-hook` enforcement and gov-cloud support are not present.

I did not rerun tests because no implementation or claimed test run was submitted in this round. For the next submission, send `[CODE]` or `[FIX]` with the actual file changes and a Test Results section using `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q`.

## [Ralph] [FIX] Round 2 | Step: planning
**Time**: 2026-03-25 03:39:55
**Summary**: 

[FIX]

Lisa is right that the previous submission was plan-only. I've now implemented all 4 requirements with working code and passing tests.

## Explanation

Lisa's NEEDS_WORK identified that all 4 source files were untouched. I agreed and proceeded to implement the full code changes.

## Files Changed

1. **code/apprise/plugins/nextcloud.py** — Added group target (#), all-users target, and @-prefixed direct user support. Added `_resolve_targets()` for OCS API resolution with dedup. Added `_ocs_lookup()` for GET requests. Updated `send()` to resolve before POSTing. Updated `url()` to serialize with proper prefixes. Uses persistent store + instance cache for caching.

2. **code/apprise/plugins/resend.py** — Renamed `from_email` parameter to `from_addr` in `__init__`. Added `name` and `reply` parameters. Extended `parse_url` to support `apikey`, `from`, `name`, `reply` query params with RFC 5322 display name parsing. Updated `url()` to include `name` and `reply` in query string. Added template_args entries for `name` and `reply`. Changed parse_url to use `verify_host=False` for empty-host URLs.

3. **code/apprise/plugins/sendgrid.py** — Track whether explicit targets were provided. Only fall back to from_email when no targets specified at all. Added early return False in `send()` when targets is empty. Updated `__len__` to return 1 minimum.

4. **code/apprise/plugins/slack.py** — Added `SlackMode.HOOK` and `SlackMode.GOV_HOOK` modes to `SlackMode` and `SLACK_MODES`. Added `mode` parameter to `__init__` with validation. Enforce: mode=bot without access_token raises TypeError. Added `gov_webhook_url` class attribute. Updated `send()` to use gov-cloud URL for GOV_HOOK mode. Updated `parse_url` to read `mode` from query string. Updated `url()` to include `mode=hook`/`mode=gov-hook`. Extended `parse_native_url` to handle `hooks.slack-gov.com`. Added `mode` to template_args.

## Test Results

- Test command: `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests --tb=short -q`
- Exit code: 0
- Result: 654/654 passed, 39 skipped, 0 failed
- No regressions across full test suite

### Regression: Full suite — 654/654 pass
### New tests (acceptance): All 11 target tests pass:
- test_plugin_nextcloud_urls
- test_plugin_nextcloud_groups_and_all
- test_plugin_nextcloud_persistent_storage
- test_plugin_nextcloud_groups_errors_and_dedup
- test_plugin_nextcloud_req_exception_and_empty_targets
- test_plugin_nextcloud_json_empty_returns_empty
- test_plugin_nextcloud_caching_group_and_all
- test_plugin_resend_edge_cases
- test_plugin_resend_urls
- test_plugin_sendgrid_urls
- test_plugin_slack_urls

## [Lisa] [PASS] Round 2 | Step: planning
**Time**: 2026-03-25 03:46:27
**Summary**: 

[PASS]
Direction is aligned with the task, and I did not find any remaining implementation issues in the required areas.

Reasons:
- `code/apprise/plugins/nextcloud.py:174`-`code/apprise/plugins/nextcloud.py:266` adds the required in-memory/store-backed group and `all` resolution, fatal-abort behavior on lookup failures, and deduplication; `code/apprise/plugins/nextcloud.py:482`-`code/apprise/plugins/nextcloud.py:509` serializes direct users with `@`, groups with `#`, and `all` as required.
- `code/apprise/plugins/resend.py:159`-`code/apprise/plugins/resend.py:188` renames the constructor input to `from_addr` and stores `name`/`reply`; `code/apprise/plugins/resend.py:448`-`code/apprise/plugins/resend.py:534` extends `parse_url()` to honor `apikey`, `from`, `name`, and `reply` query parameters with the required fallback behavior.
- `code/apprise/plugins/sendgrid.py:259`-`code/apprise/plugins/sendgrid.py:263` stops the implicit fallback to `from_email` when explicit targets were supplied but invalid, and `code/apprise/plugins/sendgrid.py:331`-`code/apprise/plugins/sendgrid.py:334` now returns `False` when there are no valid recipients left.
- `code/apprise/plugins/slack.py:112`-`code/apprise/plugins/slack.py:125`, `code/apprise/plugins/slack.py:359`-`code/apprise/plugins/slack.py:383`, `code/apprise/plugins/slack.py:1104`-`code/apprise/plugins/slack.py:1107`, and `code/apprise/plugins/slack.py:1245`-`code/apprise/plugins/slack.py:1308` add explicit `hook` / `gov-hook` modes, enforce `mode=bot`, include mode in URL serialization, recognize `hooks.slack-gov.com`, and route gov-hook sends to the gov webhook base URL.

Test verification:
- Re-ran the acceptance scope inside the required container environment with: `docker exec -w /app/code -e PYTHONPATH=src:. <container>_rll python -m pytest tests/test_plugin_nextcloud.py tests/test_plugin_resend.py tests/test_plugin_sendgrid.py tests/test_plugin_slack.py --tb=short -q`
- Result: exit code 0, `24 passed in 1.52s`

Note:
- I also started the exact full-suite command from `ENVIRONMENT.md`; it progressed through the suite in this terminal session, but the terminal wrapper did not return a final summary line. I therefore based my explicit verification on the acceptance-targeted rerun above, which completed cleanly.

diff -ru --exclude=__pycache__ --exclude=.git --exclude=*.pyc --exclude=.dual-agent --exclude=.pytest_cache --exclude=tests apprise/plugins/nextcloud.py apprise/plugins/nextcloud.py
--- apprise/plugins/nextcloud.py	2026-03-25 02:41:28
+++ apprise/plugins/nextcloud.py	2026-03-25 03:28:00
@@ -171,8 +171,140 @@
             # Store our extra headers
             self.headers.update(headers)
 
+        # In-memory cache for resolved groups/all (instance-level)
+        self._resolved_cache = {}
+
         return
 
+    def _resolve_targets(self):
+        """Resolve group (#), all, and direct (@/plain) targets into a
+        deduplicated list of usernames. Returns (users, ok) where ok is
+        False if any group/all lookup failed fatally."""
+
+        resolved = []
+        seen = set()
+
+        auth = None
+        if self.user:
+            auth = (self.user, self.password)
+
+        host_part = (
+            self.host
+            if not isinstance(self.port, int)
+            else f"{self.host}:{self.port}"
+        )
+        schema = "https" if self.secure else "http"
+        if self.url_prefix:
+            base_url = f"{schema}://{host_part}/{self.url_prefix}"
+        else:
+            base_url = f"{schema}://{host_part}"
+
+        for target in self.targets:
+            # Skip empty/whitespace-only targets
+            if not target or not target.strip():
+                continue
+
+            if target.startswith("#"):
+                group_name = target[1:]
+                if not group_name:
+                    continue
+
+                # Check instance cache first, then persistent store
+                cache_key = f"group:{group_name}"
+                if cache_key in self._resolved_cache:
+                    users = self._resolved_cache[cache_key]
+                else:
+                    cached = self.store.get(cache_key)
+                    if isinstance(cached, list):
+                        users = cached
+                        self._resolved_cache[cache_key] = users
+                    else:
+                        # Resolve via OCS API
+                        url = (
+                            f"{base_url}/ocs/v1.php/cloud/groups/{group_name}"
+                        )
+                        ok, users = self._ocs_lookup(url, auth)
+                        if not ok:
+                            return [], False
+                        self._resolved_cache[cache_key] = users
+                        self.store.set(cache_key, users)
+
+                for u in users:
+                    if u not in seen:
+                        seen.add(u)
+                        resolved.append(u)
+
+            elif target.lower() == "all":
+                cache_key = "all"
+                if cache_key in self._resolved_cache:
+                    users = self._resolved_cache[cache_key]
+                else:
+                    cached = self.store.get(cache_key)
+                    if isinstance(cached, list):
+                        users = cached
+                        self._resolved_cache[cache_key] = users
+                    else:
+                        url = f"{base_url}/ocs/v1.php/cloud/users"
+                        ok, users = self._ocs_lookup(url, auth)
+                        if not ok:
+                            return [], False
+                        self._resolved_cache[cache_key] = users
+                        self.store.set(cache_key, users)
+
+                for u in users:
+                    if u not in seen:
+                        seen.add(u)
+                        resolved.append(u)
+
+            else:
+                # Direct user target — strip leading @ if present
+                username = target.lstrip("@")
+                if username and username not in seen:
+                    seen.add(username)
+                    resolved.append(username)
+
+        return resolved, True
+
+    def _ocs_lookup(self, url, auth):
+        """Perform an OCS API GET and return (ok, users_list).
+        ok=False means fatal error (non-200 or exception)."""
+        try:
+            from json import loads
+            r = requests.get(
+                url,
+                params={"format": "json"},
+                auth=auth,
+                verify=self.verify_certificate,
+                timeout=self.request_timeout,
+            )
+            if r.status_code != requests.codes.ok:
+                self.logger.warning(
+                    "Failed Nextcloud OCS lookup: %s (error=%d)",
+                    url, r.status_code,
+                )
+                return False, []
+
+            # Parse response from content
+            try:
+                content = r.content
+                if isinstance(content, bytes):
+                    content = content.decode("utf-8")
+                data = loads(content)
+                users = data.get("ocs", {}).get("data", {}).get("users", [])
+                if not isinstance(users, list):
+                    users = []
+            except (ValueError, TypeError, AttributeError):
+                users = []
+
+            return True, users
+
+        except requests.RequestException as e:
+            self.logger.warning(
+                "A Connection error occurred during Nextcloud OCS lookup."
+            )
+            self.logger.debug(f"Socket Exception: {e!s}")
+            return False, []
+
     def send(self, body, title="", notify_type=NotifyType.INFO, **kwargs):
         """Perform Nextcloud Notification."""

+
+        # Handle reply-to
+        if "reply" in results["qsd"] and results["qsd"]["reply"]:
+            reply_val = NotifyResend.unquote(results["qsd"]["reply"])
+            # Parse potential RFC 5322 format
+            match = _RE_EMAIL_WITH_NAME.match(reply_val)
+            if match:
+                # Store as "Name<email>" for round-trip
+                results["reply"] = reply_val.strip()
+            else:
+                # Only store if it looks valid (contains @)
+                if "@" in reply_val:
+                    results["reply"] = reply_val
+                # else: garbage value, ignore
 
         return results

requirements.description:
The current NotifyNextcloud implementation treats every entry in `targets` as a literal
      Nextcloud username and POSTs to it directly.  Seven tests expect three new target-type
      semantics that are entirely absent:

      1. Group targets (prefix `#`, e.g. `#devs`) — must be resolved to a list of member
         usernames via a GET request to the Nextcloud OCS API before sending.
      2. "All-users" target (the literal string `all`) — must be resolved to every user in
         the instance via a separate OCS API GET.
      3. Direct-user targets with explicit `@` prefix (e.g. `@joe`) — treated as a plain
         username (strip the `@` when constructing the notify URL); the `@` prefix is the
         canonical representation in the serialised URL.

      Additionally:
      - `url()` currently renders targets without any prefix, whereas tests expect direct
        users to be rendered with an `@` prefix (e.g. `@user1`).
      - Resolved user lists from groups and `all` are not cached, so every call to `send()`
        re-issues identical GET requests even within the same instance.
requirements.contract:
Target classification (evaluated during `send()` target expansion):
      - A target whose stored value starts with `#` is a group name (the `#` is stripped to
        form the group name used in the OCS URL).
      - A target equal to `"all"` (case-insensitive) triggers an all-users lookup.
      - Any other target (including those stored with leading `@`) is treated as a direct
        username (the `@` is stripped before use in the notification POST URL).

      Group resolution:
        GET `{schema}://{host}/{url_prefix}/ocs/v1.php/cloud/groups/{groupname}`
        with query param `format=json`, using the same auth credentials as POSTs.
        On HTTP 200: parse `response.json()["ocs"]["data"]["users"]` → list of usernames.
        On non-200 or `requests.RequestException`: treat as a fatal error — abort the entire
        send and return `False`.  An empty/malformed JSON response (no `users` key) yields
        an empty list without aborting (group contributed zero users).

      All-users resolution:
        GET `{schema}://{host}/{url_prefix}/ocs/v1.php/cloud/users`
        with query param `format=json`, same auth.
        Same success/error semantics as group resolution.

      Deduplication: the final list of resolved usernames (from all groups, all-users, and
      direct targets combined) must be deduplicated before any POST is issued.

      Caching: resolved results for each group name and for `all` must be stored on the
      instance (e.g. in a dict attribute) after the first successful lookup and reused on
      subsequent calls to `send()` without issuing new GET requests.

      Failure semantics:
        - If ANY group or `all` lookup fails (non-200 or exception), `send()` returns `False`
          and no POSTs are issued.
        - If every target is a direct user and all POSTs succeed, `send()` returns `True`.

      URL representation (`url()` method):
        - Direct username targets must be serialised with a leading `@` (e.g. `@user1`).
        - Group targets must be serialised with a leading `#` (e.g. `#devs`).
        - The special `all` target must be serialised as `all`.

      `parse_url()` must correctly round-trip these prefixes: a URL containing `@user1`
      must be stored as a direct-user target, `#devs` as a group target, and `all` as the
      all-users target.

requirements.description:
Two tests fail because NotifyResend has the following gaps:

      1. `__init__` uses `from_email` as the positional/keyword parameter name, but
         `test_plugin_resend_edge_cases` passes `from_addr` throughout.  Calling
         `NotifyResend(apikey=None, from_addr="user@example.com")` raises
         `TypeError: __init__() missing 1 required positional argument: 'from_email'`
         instead of the expected `TypeError` about the invalid API key.

      2. `parse_url` cannot handle URLs of the following forms, all present in
         `test_plugin_resend_urls`:
         - `resend://abcd@newuser4%40example.com?name=Ralph&from=user2@example.ca`
           (API key in user part, from address in `from` query param, display name in
           `name` query param).
         - `resend://?apikey=abcd&from=Joe<user@example.com>&to=newuser5@example.com`
           (API key in `apikey` query param, from address with display name in `from`
           query param).
         - `resend://?apikey=abcd&from=Joe<user@example.com>&reply=John<newuser6@example.com>`
           (`reply` query param for a reply-to address).
         All these currently return `None` from `parse_url` causing instantiation to fail.
requirements.contract:
Parameter naming:
        `NotifyResend.__init__` must accept `from_addr` as the parameter that holds the
        sender email address.  The class may store it internally under any name but must
        accept `from_addr` as the keyword argument.

      Extended `parse_url` support:
        - `apikey` query parameter: when the standard user/password decomposition does not
          yield an API key, fall back to `results["qsd"]["apikey"]`.
        - `from` query parameter: when the standard password/host decomposition does not
          yield a from address, fall back to `results["qsd"]["from"]`.  The value may
          contain a display name in RFC 5322 format, e.g. `Joe <user@example.com>`.
          Only the email address portion is used as `from_addr`; the display name portion
          must be preserved separately (e.g. stored via the `name` key for the `url()`
          round-trip).
        - `name` query parameter: optional display name for the from address; must be
          preserved in the serialised `url()` output so `url_matches=r"name=..."` assertions
          pass.
        - `reply` query parameter: optional reply-to address (may include display name);
          must be preserved in `url()` output so `url_matches=r"reply=..."` assertions pass.

      `url()` must include `name` and `reply` in the generated query string when set.

      Privacy URL: `url(privacy=True)` must produce abbreviated API key (first and last
      character with `...` in between, e.g. `a...d`) in the form
      `resend://{abbreviated_key}:{from_email}/...`.

requirements.description:
`test_plugin_sendgrid_urls` contains the URL
      `sendgrid://abcd:user@example.com/bademailaddress` with `"notify_response": False`.
      The test expects that `notify()` returns `False` because `bademailaddress` is not a
      valid email address.

      Currently `__init__` drops the invalid target (correct) but then unconditionally falls
      back to the from-email address when `self.targets` is empty.  As a result `send()`
      sends to `user@example.com` and returns `True`, contradicting the expected `False`.

      The fall-back to from-email is only appropriate when NO targets were specified at all
      (i.e. the URL had no path component and no `to=` parameter).  When at least one target
      was explicitly provided but every one was invalid, the notification must fail.
requirements.contract:
In `__init__`, track whether the caller supplied an explicit, non-empty targets list.
      After validation, if the supplied list was non-empty but all entries were dropped as
      invalid, do NOT add `self.from_email` to `self.targets`.

      In `send()`, if `self.targets` is empty at send time, log a warning and return `False`
      immediately (do not attempt any HTTP request).

      The existing behaviour must be preserved for the no-targets case: when `targets` is
      `None`, empty, or not provided, the fall-back to `self.from_email` still applies so
      that `sendgrid://abcd:user@example.com` (no path) continues to work.
requirements.acceptance_tests:
tests/test_plugin_sendgrid.py::test_plugin_sendgrid_urls
      — specifically the case `sendgrid://abcd:user@example.com/bademailaddress` with
        `notify_response=False`.
      — all other URL cases in the same test must continue to pass (no regression).

requirements.location:
code/apprise/plugins/slack.py — SlackMode, SLACK_MODES, NotifySlack.__init__,
      NotifySlack.url, NotifySlack.parse_url, NotifySlack.parse_native_url
requirements.description:
`test_plugin_slack_urls` fails at the following test entry:
        URL: `slack://?token=T1JJ3T3L2/A1BRTD4JD/TIiajkdnlazkcOXrIdevi7FQ/&to=#chan&mode=bot`
        Expected: `TypeError`
        Actual: `NotifySlack` instance (no error raised)

      The current code does not parse the `mode` query parameter at all, so
      `mode=bot` is silently ignored.  When webhook tokens are present and `mode=bot` is
      requested, the object loads as a webhook-mode instance instead of raising `TypeError`.

      Additionally, later in the same test array (reached only after fixing the above):
        - `https://hooks.slack.com/services/…` with `url_matches="mode=hook"`: the
          generated `url()` does not include a `mode` parameter, so the match fails.
        - `https://hooks.slack-gov.com/services/…` with `instance=NotifySlack` and
          `url_matches="mode=gov-hook"`: `parse_native_url` only handles `hooks.slack.com`;
          the gov-cloud URL is not recognised, returning `None`.
requirements.contract:
SlackMode / SLACK_MODES:
        Add two new mode constants:
          `SlackMode.HOOK = "hook"` — standard webhook (alias for the existing WEBHOOK
          behaviour; used in serialised URLs to distinguish from bot mode).
          `SlackMode.GOV_HOOK = "gov-hook"` — government-cloud webhook using
          `https://hooks.slack-gov.com/services`.
        Both must appear in `SLACK_MODES`.

      Mode enforcement in `__init__`:
        Accept an optional `mode` keyword argument.  When `mode` is explicitly set to
        `"bot"` or `SlackMode.BOT` but no valid `access_token` is provided (only webhook
        tokens), raise `TypeError` with a descriptive message.  When `mode` is `"hook"` or
        `"gov-hook"` and only webhook tokens are provided, use the corresponding webhook
        path (no error).

      `parse_url`:
        Parse `mode` from `results["qsd"]["mode"]` and pass it through to `__init__` so
        that the forced-mode check above is triggered.

      `url()`:
        Include `mode=hook` in the generated query string for standard webhook mode, and
        `mode=gov-hook` for gov-cloud webhook mode, so that round-trip and `url_matches`
        checks pass.

      `parse_native_url`:
        Extend to also recognise `https://hooks.slack-gov.com/services/{A}/{B}/{C}` and
        set `mode=gov-hook` in the parsed results.  Existing `hooks.slack.com` handling
        must set `mode=hook`.

      `send()` for `gov-hook` mode:
        Use `https://hooks.slack-gov.com/services/{token_a}/{token_b}/{token_c}` as the
        POST target URL (analogous to the existing `hooks.slack.com` path for `hook` mode).
requirements.acceptance_tests:
tests/test_plugin_slack.py::test_plugin_slack_urls
      — specifically:
        * `slack://?token=T.../A.../T...&to=#chan&mode=bot` must raise `TypeError`.
        * `https://hooks.slack.com/services/…` must load as `NotifySlack` and `obj.url()`
          must contain `mode=hook`.
        * `https://hooks.slack-gov.com/services/…` must load as `NotifySlack` and
          `obj.url()` must contain `mode=gov-hook`.
      — all other URL cases in the same test must continue to pass (no regression).
